license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d5k84v
title: Installation and Getting Started
author: Afiahayati

steps_raw.instruction: Download source code from here , and decompress the tar ball. http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/src/ ~$ tar zxvf MataVelvet-v1.1.01.tgz decompress tar ball

Alternatively, up-to-date source code is available from github MetaVelvet project . https://github.com/hacchy/MetaVelvet ~$ git clone git://github.com/hacchy/MetaVelvet.git git clone MetaVelvet project
Change directory to the MetaVelvet directory. ~$ cd MetaVelvet-v1.1.01 moving into MetaVelvet directory

Compile MetaVelvet execution files. ~/MetaVelvet-v1.1.01$ make ['MAXKMERLENGTH = k'] ['CATEGORIES = cat'] k - the maximum k-mer size you like to use.cat - the maximum number of read categories (i.e., maximum number of libraries in different insert lengths) ~/MetaVelvet-v1.1.01$ make ['MAXKMERLENGTH = k'] ['CATEGORIES = cat'] k - the maximum k-mer size you like to use. cat - the maximum number of read categories (i.e., maximum number of libraries in different insert lengths)
Then, an executable file, meta-velvetg will be created (>= 1.1.01).
Copy the two executable files to /usr/bin or a directory you like to install. ~/MetaVelvet-v1.1.01$ cp meta-velvetg /usr/bin/ copy executable files to /usr/bin
MetaVelvet (>= 1.1.01) uses velveth and velvetg outputs for metagenomics assembly. Three files ("Sequences", "Roadmaps", and "Graph2") are needed for running meta-velvetg .
Download a small dataset from here , and decompress the tar ball. http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/data/ ~$ tar zxvf HMP.small.tar.gz decompress

Execute velveth to import read sequences and construct k -mer hash table. Please check that " out-dir /Sequences" and " out-dir /Roadmaps" files are created. Note that k -mer size has important effect on assembly results. If your short read data is longer than 65bp, we recommend k -mer longer than 51. ~$ velveth out-dir 51 \ -fasta -shortPaired \ HMP.small/SRR041654_shuffled.fasta \ HMP.small/SRR041655_shuffled.fasta

Execute velvetg to construct an initial de Bruijin graph. Please check that " out-dir /Graph2" file is created. Please use -exp_cov auto option, otherwise meta-velvetg cannot be executed. ~$ velvetg out-dir -exp_cov auto -ins_length 260 executing velvetg

Execute meta-velvetg to output scaffold sequences. Please check that " out-dir /meta-velvetg.contigs.fa" file is created. The FASTA file is the main assembling results. ~$ meta-velvetg out-dir [-ins_length 260] | tee logfile Executing meta-velvetg
